communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records from January 1, 2021 through the date of search sufficient to identify, describe, and locate existing Bureau of Transportation Statistics records, datasets, repositories, tables, files, archives, data lake/lakehouse tables, object-store locations, or other electronic records repositories that store historical FAA System Wide Information Management (SWIM) data in source-faithful or technically normalized form.

This request applies to records maintained by BTS, OST-R, the Volpe National Transportation Systems Center, contractors, cloud service providers, or other entities to the extent those records are maintained for BTS or are otherwise agency records subject to FOIA.

For purposes of this request, “source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM data” means SWIM messages, payloads, records, files, or tables that preserve the substantive informational content of FAA SWIM messages substantially as published by or received from FAA SWIM services.

This includes repositories where SWIM messages have been technically normalized, flattened, parsed, mapped, or converted for storage or retrieval, including conversion from XML, JSON, or other SWIM message formats into Parquet, Avro, CSV, database tables, Delta Lake tables, partitioned files, or other machine-readable storage formats.

A repository is within the scope of this request even if some transport-layer, envelope, header, routing, broker, or ingestion metadata was not retained, provided that the repository preserves the substantive SWIM payload fields or message content in a source-faithful form.

This request includes repositories where SWIM data has been minimally processed for technical storage or retrieval, including serialization, deserialization, compression, decompression, schema validation, parsing, flattening, field mapping, type conversion, timestamp normalization, deduplication, indexing, partitioning, topic tagging, source-service tagging, or conversion to columnar or tabular formats.

This request includes only the following categories of records:

1. dataset inventories, table lists, file inventories, object-store inventories, metadata catalog entries, data catalog records, or other records identifying BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, or cloud-hosted repositories containing source-faithful or technically normalized historical FAA SWIM data;

2. schemas, data dictionaries, field definitions, table definitions, topic lists, service lists, message-type lists, XML schemas, source-to-target mappings, transformation mappings, and records identifying native storage formats for those repositories;

3. records identifying the source SWIM service, feed, topic, message type, producer system, ingestion timestamp, message timestamp, source message identifier if retained, partition key, storage location, retention period, or export method for those repositories;

4. retention schedules, archive policies, deletion policies, records-management documentation, and data lifecycle documentation for those repositories;

5. architecture diagrams, ingestion diagrams, pipeline diagrams, interface control documents, data flow diagrams, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, and system design documents showing FAA SWIM data being ingested into BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, cloud-hosted, data lake, lakehouse, database, or analytics environments, but only to the extent those records identify or describe source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM repositories;

6. existing export procedures, query procedures, APIs, standard operating procedures, scripts, jobs, notebooks, or documentation used to retrieve historical records from those repositories.

This request includes source-faithful or technically normalized repositories associated with FAA SWIM services or feeds including SFDPS, En Route Flight Data Publication or Query, En Route Airspace Data Publication or Query, En Route General Message Publication or Query, En Route Operational Data Publication or Query, STDDS, TFMS/TFMData, TBFM, TFDM, ITWS, FNS/NOTAM, and other FAA SWIM services, to the extent such records are maintained by or for BTS.

This request does not seek downstream analytical, curated, modeled, fused, enriched, aggregated, scored, machine-learning, reporting, dashboard, business-intelligence, performance-measurement, delay-attribution, commercial-flight-database output, public-statistics, or decision-support datasets merely because they use SWIM as an input.

The distinction intended by this request is between source-faithful technical normalizations, which are included, and downstream analytical derivations, which are excluded.

Please do not process downstream derived products unless they are necessary to identify the existence, location, schema, retention period, format, or exportability of source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM repositories.

I am not requesting live operational access, credentials, passwords, private keys, internal network information, or operational security procedures. I am not asking DOT, BTS, OST-R, Volpe, or any contractor to create a new analytical product, write a new computer program, or perform analysis. I seek existing records, metadata, schemas, documentation, inventories, and export/query procedures maintained in the ordinary course.

If this request is considered broad, please process first records identifying any BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, or cloud-hosted landing-zone, staging, bronze-layer, source table, message archive, query archive, replay store, object-store path, Parquet table, Delta Lake table, or equivalent repository that stores FAA SWIM data before downstream analytical transformation.

Please produce responsive records in native electronic format where readily reproducible, including associated metadata. If native format is not readily reproducible, please produce records in a machine-readable format such as CSV, JSON, XML, Parquet, or PDF for documentation records.

Please process this request on a rolling basis. As responsive records are located, reviewed, and cleared for release, please produce them in interim batches rather than waiting until all potentially responsive records have been processed.

If any portion is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions and identify the exemption asserted for each withholding. If DOT/BTS believes any portion of this request is unclear or overbroad, please contact me before denying or closing the request so that I may narrow it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records from January 1, 2021 through the date of search sufficient to identify, describe, and locate existing FAA repositories that store historical FAA System Wide Information Management (SWIM) data in raw or source-faithful form.

For purposes of this request, “raw or source-faithful SWIM data” means SWIM messages, payloads, records, or tables that preserve the substantive informational content of FAA SWIM messages substantially as published by or received from FAA SWIM services.

This includes repositories where SWIM messages have been technically normalized, flattened, parsed, mapped, or converted for storage or retrieval, including conversion from XML, JSON, or other message formats into Parquet, Avro, CSV, database tables, Delta Lake tables, partitioned files, or other machine-readable storage formats.

A repository is still within the scope of this request even if some transport-layer, envelope, header, routing, broker, or ingestion metadata was not retained, provided that the repository preserves the substantive SWIM payload fields or message content in a source-faithful form.

This request includes repositories where SWIM data has been minimally processed for technical storage or retrieval, including serialization, deserialization, compression, decompression, schema validation, parsing, flattening, field mapping, type conversion, timestamp normalization, deduplication, indexing, partitioning, topic tagging, source-service tagging, or conversion to columnar or tabular formats.

This request does not include downstream datasets whose primary purpose is analytical transformation, enrichment, joining with non-SWIM sources, aggregation, scoring, modeling, reporting, dashboarding, business intelligence, machine learning, delay attribution, performance measurement, or decision support. The distinction intended by this request is between source-faithful technical normalizations, which is included, and downstream analytical derivations, which is excluded.

This request is limited to records concerning repositories such as message archives, query archives, replay stores, landing zones, data lakes, staging tables, bronze-layer tables, object-store paths, database tables, files, or partitions that preserve SWIM data substantially as received.

This request includes only the following categories of records:

1. dataset inventories, table lists, file inventories, object-store inventories, metadata catalog entries, or other records identifying repositories containing raw or source-faithful historical SWIM data;

2. schemas, data dictionaries, field definitions, topic lists, service lists, message-type lists, XML schemas, source-to-target mappings, and records identifying native storage formats for those repositories;

3. records identifying source SWIM service, topic, message type, producer system, ingestion timestamp, message timestamp, source message identifier, partition key, retention period, storage location, or export method for those repositories;

4. retention schedules, archive policies, deletion policies, and records-management documentation for those repositories;

5. existing export procedures, query procedures, APIs, standard operating procedures, scripts, jobs, or documentation used to retrieve historical records from those repositories.

This request specifically includes raw or source-faithful repositories associated with SFDPS, En Route Flight Data Publication or Query, En Route Airspace Data Publication or Query, En Route General Message Publication or Query, En Route Operational Data Publication or Query, STDDS, TFMS/TFMData, TBFM, TFDM, ITWS, FNS/NOTAM, and other FAA SWIM services.

This request does not seek downstream analytical, curated, modeled, fused, enriched, aggregated, scored, machine-learning, reporting, dashboard, business-intelligence, performance-measurement, delay-attribution, or decision-support datasets merely because they use SWIM as an input. Please do not process downstream derived products unless they are necessary to identify the existence, location, schema, retention period, format, or exportability of raw or source-faithful SWIM repositories.

I am not requesting live operational access, credentials, passwords, private keys, internal network information, or operational security procedures. I am not asking FAA to create a new analytical product, write a new computer program, or perform analysis. I seek existing records, metadata, schemas, documentation, inventories, and export/query procedures maintained in the ordinary course.

Please produce responsive records in native electronic format where readily reproducible, including associated metadata. If native format is not readily reproducible, please produce records in a machine-readable format such as CSV, JSON, XML, Parquet, or PDF for documentation records.

Please process this request on a rolling basis. As responsive records are located, reviewed, and cleared for release, please produce them in interim batches rather than waiting until all potentially responsive records have been processed.

If any portion is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions and identify the exemption asserted for each withholding. If FAA believes any portion of this request is unclear or overbroad, please contact me before denying or closing the request so that I may narrow it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg
